Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given a process tree that currently is a sequence of 1 Create Purchase Requisition 2 Approve Purchase Requisition 3 A parallel block AND containing Create Purchase Order Receive Goods Receive Invoice Match Invoice to PO Pay Invoice 4 Close Case The problem is that the parallel block allows the activities to occur in any order which is not correct for the domain

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The standard Procure to Pay process 1 Create Purchase Requisition 2 Approve Purchase Requisition 3 Create Purchase Order after approval 4 Receive Goods after the PO is sent to the supplier but note sometimes goods are received before the invoice sometimes after 5 Receive Invoice can be before or after receiving goods but must be before matching and payment 6 Match Invoice to PO must be after both the PO exists and the invoice is received 7 Pay Invoice after matching 8 Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: However note that steps 4 and 5 Receive Goods and Receive Invoice can occur in any order relative to each other but both must be completed before step 6 Match Invoice to PO Also step 3 Create Purchase Order must be done before step 4 and step 5 because you cannot receive goods or an invoice without a PO

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: So a more accurate ordering is create req to approve req to create po to receive goods AND receive invoice to match invoice to pay invoice to close case

Name: Validation - PE
Text: But note the matching step requires both the goods and invoice to be received and the PO to be created However the goods and invoice can be received in any order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore we can structure the process tree as Sequence create req approve req create po Parallel receive goods receive invoice match invoice pay invoice close case

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This ensures The requisition is created and approved first Then the PO is created Then we have a parallel block for receiving goods and invoice both must occur in any order Then we match the invoice which requires both the goods and invoice to be received and the PO to exist Then we pay the invoice Then we close the case

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This prevents unwanted behavior such as Paying the invoice before receiving goods or invoice Matching the invoice before receiving goods or invoice Receiving goods or invoice before creating the PO
